perturbation.original_text: 6. CONFIDENTIALITY 6.1 CONFIDENTIALITY INFORMATION. Each party (the "RECEIVING PARTY") acknowledges that by reason of its relationship to the other party (the "DISCLOSING PARTY") hereunder, the Receiving Party will have access to certain information and materials, including the terms of this Agreement, concerning the Disclosing Party's business, plans, technology, products and services that are confidential and of substantial value to the Disclosing Party, which value would be impaired if such information were disclosed to third parties ("CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"). The Receiving Party agrees that it shall not use in any way for its own account or the account of any third party, nor disclose to any third party, any such Confidential Information revealed to it by the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall take every reasonable precaution to protect the confidentiality of Confidential Information. Upon request by the Receiving Party, the Disclosing Party shall advise whether or not it considers any particular information to be Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall not publish any technical description of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information beyond any descriptions published by the Disclosing party. In the event of expiration or termination of this Agreement, there shall be no use or disclosure by the Receiving Party of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and the Receiving Party shall not develop any software, devices, components or assemblies utilizing the Disclosing Party's Intellectual Property. Both parties agree that the terms and conditions of this Agreement are confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party, unless disclosure is compelled by final, non-appealable order of a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. CONFIDENTIALITY 6.1 CONFIDENTIALITY INFORMATION. Each party (the "RECEIVING PARTY") acknowledges that by reason of its relationship to the other party (the "DISCLOSING PARTY") hereunder, the Receiving Party will have access to certain information and materials, including the terms of this Agreement, concerning the Disclosing Party's business, plans, technology, products and services that are confidential and of substantial value to the Disclosing Party, which value would be impaired if such information were disclosed to third parties ("CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"). The Receiving Party agrees that it may use Confidential Information for its own account or the account of any third party, and disclose to any third party, any such Confidential Information revealed to it by the Disclosing Party, if such use or disclosure furthers the objectives of this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall take every reasonable precaution to protect the confidentiality of Confidential Information. Upon request by the Receiving Party, the Disclosing Party shall advise whether or not it considers any particular information to be Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall not publish any technical description of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information beyond any descriptions published by the Disclosing party. In the event of expiration or termination of this Agreement, there shall be no use or disclosure by the Receiving Party of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and the Receiving Party shall not develop any software, devices, components or assemblies utilizing the Disclosing Party's Intellectual Property. Both parties agree that the terms and conditions of this Agreement are confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party, unless disclosure is compelled by final, non-appealable order of a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text in section 6.1 introduces a direct contradiction to the initial restriction on the use of Confidential Information. The original text clearly states that the Receiving Party shall not use or disclose Confidential Information, whereas the edited version allows for such use and disclosure 'if such use or disclosure furthers the objectives of this Agreement.' This creates ambiguity and conflict regarding what constitutes acceptable use of Confidential Information, making the enforcement of confidentiality problematic. It's unclear under what conditions the Receiving Party can leverage this exception.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. CONFIDENTIALITY 6.1 CONFIDENTIALITY INFORMATION. Each party (the "RECEIVING PARTY") acknowledges that by reason of its relationship to the other party (the "DISCLOSING PARTY") hereunder, the Receiving Party will have access to certain information and materials, including the terms of this Agreement, concerning the Disclosing Party's business, plans, technology, products and services that are confidential and of substantial value to the Disclosing Party, which value would be impaired if such information were disclosed to third parties ("CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"). The Receiving Party agrees that it may use Confidential Information for its own account or the account of any third party, and disclose to any third party, any such Confidential Information revealed to it by the Disclosing Party, if such use or disclosure furthers the objectives of this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall take every reasonable precaution to protect the confidentiality of Confidential Information. Upon request by the Receiving Party, the Disclosing Party shall advise whether or not it considers any particular information to be Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall not publish any technical description of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information beyond any descriptions published by the Disclosing party. In the event of expiration or termination of this Agreement, there shall be no use or disclosure by the Receiving Party of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and the Receiving Party shall not develop any software, devices, components or assemblies utilizing the Disclosing Party's Intellectual Property. Both parties agree that the terms and conditions of this Agreement are confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party, unless disclosure is compelled by final, non-appealable order of a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6.1 directly contradicts the original intent of the confidentiality clause. The original text imposes a strict prohibition on the Receiving Party using or disclosing Confidential Information. The modified text introduces an exception, allowing use and disclosure 'if such use or disclosure furthers the objectives of this Agreement.' This exception creates a significant contradiction, as it's now unclear when the Receiving Party is actually prohibited from using or disclosing the information, undermining the confidentiality provisions outlined in the same section and elsewhere in the document.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 ASSIGNMENT. This Agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefits of the parties hereto and their permitted successors and assigns. Reseller may nat assign or otherwise transfer this Agreement without MediaNet Group Technologies's prior written consent except to a successor.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 ASSIGNMENT. This Agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefits of the parties hereto and their permitted successors and assigns. Reseller may assign or otherwise transfer this Agreement without MediaNet Group Technologies's prior written consent except to a successor that is directly competing with MediaNet Group Technologies.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the clause to state that Reseller may assign or transfer the agreement without consent except to a competitor, while the original requires MediaNet Group Technologies's prior written consent to assign or transfer this Agreement except to a successor, introduces a contradiction. The original provides that Reseller needs permission to transfer to assign the contract, while in the new clause, there is permission but not when it is competitor, creating a contradiction when assigning or transferring a contact can be made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.1 ASSIGNMENT. This Agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefits of the parties hereto and their permitted successors and assigns. Reseller may assign or otherwise transfer this Agreement without MediaNet Group Technologies's prior written consent except to a successor that is directly competing with MediaNet Group Technologies.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Reseller may *not* assign or transfer the agreement without MediaNet's written consent (except to a successor). The changed text modifies this, stating that the Reseller *may* assign or transfer the agreement without consent, *except* to a competitor of MediaNet. The contradiction arises because the original text unequivocally requires consent for any assignment (other than to a successor), while the modified text allows assignment without consent to entities that are *not* competitors, thus creating a direct conflict within the agreement regarding when consent is required for assignment. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, highlighting the direct conflict in the terms governing assignment. This exemplifies a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.
